items.title: Revamped Email Marketing Workbench in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: The email marketing admin interface has been completely redesigned to function as a cohesive operator workbench. Key features include a bilingual email composer, a comprehensive campaign lifecycle view, and enhanced metrics for audience and template readiness. This update ensures a consistent user experience with improved accessibility and responsive design.

items.title: Enhanced Security for Kling Logs in Platform Service 🔒
items.summary: This update improves security by redacting sensitive upstream credentials from logs related to Kling video, motion, lip-sync, and talking-photo functionalities. Key changes include enhanced error handling in the BaseHandler and the introduction of safe logging methods to prevent credential exposure during debugging. These modifications ensure that essential debugging information is retained without compromising security.

items.title: Enhance Omni Reference UI in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a streamlined canonical `kling-o1` model with new Omni image and video reference controls. Key features include multi-image uploads, reference-video selection, and enhanced user interface translations across 17 locales. The changes improve user experience and ensure compliance with new submission requirements.

items.title: Update Kling Model Identifier in Clis 🚀
items.summary: This update reflects the upstream Docs OpenAPI specification change, renaming the Kling O1 model identifier from `kling-video-o1` to `kling-o1`. Key modifications include updates to the `KLING_MODELS` list and the models table in the README. These changes ensure consistency across the CLI and documentation.

items.title: Enhance Omni SDK with Canonical Contracts in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the canonical `kling-o1` model across both Python and TypeScript SDKs, enhancing the functionality with typed contracts for camera, image, and video references. Key improvements include the removal of unsafe elements and unknown-field forwarding, alongside strict type checks and new tests for serialization. The rollout is contingent on the deployment of related platform services to ensure seamless integration.

items.title: Enhance Kling Tool with Canonical References in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes the canonical `kling-o1` model ID in the Dify Kling tool, improving the handling of Omni references. Key changes include the addition of typed and sanitized `image_list` and `video_list` forwarding, along with strict validation constraints for model requests. The plugin will only be published after completing necessary dependencies, ensuring a smooth rollout.

items.title: Publish Canonical Omni Guide in A P Is 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the canonical `kling-o1` model name and outlines the five-second constraint. It documents various Omni request shapes, including multi-image and video-edit requests, while also addressing forbidden combinations and tenant-safe replacements. Historical claims regarding the Extend model have been corrected for accuracy.

items.title: Enhance Omni References Documentation in Skills 📚
items.summary: This update documents the canonical `kling-o1` and V3 Omni image/video reference workflows, detailing parameters such as O1 duration and media-format constraints. It also removes the `element_list` parameter for tenant safety and completes the core `/kling/videos` parameter table. Validation has been performed with all tests passing, ensuring the integrity of the changes.

items.title: Enhance Omni Tools with Canonical Updates in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the legacy O1 identifier with the new canonical `kling-o1`, enhancing the model's reliability. It introduces typed Pydantic schemas for `image_list` and `video_list`, while also improving camera control validation. The README and changelog have been aligned with the new model catalog, ensuring clear documentation for users.

items.title: Enable Remote Debugging by Default in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update changes the default setting for the remote debugging endpoint to enabled, allowing for easier remote operations on Wisdom appliances. The endpoint remains token-gated, ensuring that only users with a valid API token can access it. Key files updated include configuration settings, API routes, and test cases to reflect this change.

items.title: Launch of Canonical Kling Model in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the unlaunched `kling-video-o1` identifier with the canonical `kling-o1` across various components, including billing and OpenAPI. It introduces structured contracts for image and video lists while ensuring O1 billing is limited to specific generation types. Additionally, OpenAPI types have been corrected, enhancing overall API accuracy and reliability.

items.title: Enhance Omni Routing with Canonical Model in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the canonical public O1 model ID 'kling-o1' and maps it to ephone's legacy provider ID. Key improvements include strict model validation, immediate availability of O1 and V3 Omni references, and enhanced routing capabilities. Additionally, tenant-unsafe elements are rejected, and reference payloads are sanitized to ensure security and reliability.

items.title: Enhance Tool Result Serialization in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a centralized serialization method for structured tool results, ensuring that compact JSON outputs are preserved even when pretty JSON exceeds character limits. Key changes include the addition of a new serialization function and modifications to existing orchestration and streaming executor files. The implementation has been validated with extensive tests, confirming that results are accurately managed across different execution paths.

items.title: Revamp Xiaohongshu Note Card Reconstruction in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the reconstruction of Xiaohongshu list cards by implementing a deterministic scan for named links and bounded ranges. It addresses issues such as ignoring empty duplicates and reporting ambiguous authors without guessing. The changes ensure a more reliable and accurate representation of note cards, improving the overall functionality of the Skills module.

items.title: Enhance Search Result Stability in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the reliability of search result clicks by implementing a secondary validation check on the selected cell's name before proceeding. If the cell has been rebound to a different result, the click is skipped, preventing incorrect actions. The changes include new tests to ensure the functionality works as intended without impacting existing interactions.

items.title: Enhance Homepage Story Headings for Better Visibility in Nexior ✨
items.summary: This update emphasizes the homepage business-story headings by applying a bolder font weight of 800 while maintaining the existing semantic structure and responsive sizes. The changes ensure that the headings stand out without affecting the overall design or functionality. Validation checks confirm that the adjustments are clean and consistent across various devices.

items.title: Revamp Home to Showcase AI Business Platform in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The home page has been completely rebuilt to highlight the full capabilities of the AI business platform while maintaining Nexior's dark brand aesthetic. Key features include a detailed capability catalog, functional hero callouts, and improved responsive design. The update also enhances localization support and ensures all assets are CDN-hosted for optimal performance.

items.title: Refined Referral Risk Admin Page in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Referral Risk Admin page by aligning it with adjacent console pages, implementing a bold title and Element Plus card styling. Key improvements include the removal of duplicate padding, the addition of a dedicated warning icon, and ensuring responsive filters for both desktop and mobile views. Comprehensive validation checks confirm the visual consistency and functionality across various screen sizes.

items.title: Enhance Browser Upgrade Guidance Visibility in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that interactive Browser Skills remain visible even when no eligible BrowserDevice is selected, providing users with necessary update guidance. Additionally, browser tools are now registered one capability at a time, contingent on both connector policy and device capabilities. The changes also maintain strict filtering for scheduled and unattended executions, while keeping certain tools unavailable for older devices.

items.title: Enhance Wisdom Endpoint Resilience in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the resilience of the single-tenant Wisdom endpoint by allowing it to remain ready during transient login and OCR stalls for up to two minutes. The failure threshold for the readiness probe has been increased from 2 to 12, preventing premature removal of the endpoint and reducing 502/503 errors during brief outages. Validation tests confirm that the changes maintain overall system stability while enhancing user experience.

items.title: Enhance Wisdom API Stability and Recovery in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update stabilizes the Wisdom API post-login by preventing duplicate auto-start instances and allowing a recovery window for running APIs. Key changes include improved error handling for API crashes, a new PowerShell console for live logging, and adjustments to the WeChat UI interaction to avoid unnecessary refresh tasks. Overall, these enhancements aim to ensure a more reliable and responsive user experience.

items.title: Eliminate Cookie Reset Functionality in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update removes the `clear_cookies` capability from various components, including the runtime serializer and connector allowlist. The historical migration remains intact, ensuring that existing data is preserved. Additionally, connector synchronization now correctly overwrites live policies with the supported capabilities.

items.title: Streamline Extension by Removing Cookie Reset in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `browser.clear_cookies` functionality from the extension's capabilities, ensuring a more secure user experience. It also restores the backward-compatible optional `browser.key.ref` and releases the extension version 0.6.1. All tests have passed successfully, confirming that the changes do not affect other functionalities like file uploads.

items.title: Streamline Browser Capabilities by Removing Cookie Reset in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `clear_cookies` capability from the browser tool and related tests, enhancing the clarity of browser functionalities. The changes include modifications to several files, ensuring that the upload functionality remains intact while simplifying the overall browser capabilities. All tests have been validated, confirming no errors and maintaining compatibility with existing features.

items.title: Streamline Xiaohongshu Account Management in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the `clear_cookies` functionality from the Xiaohongshu frontmatter and browser tool instructions, opting instead for visible logout and switch-account controls for account resets. The changes ensure that the parseable frontmatter fix from the previous update is preserved, maintaining all publishing workflows. Validation confirms that all skills tests passed successfully without the cookie reset feature.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Skill Frontmatter Parsing in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the frontmatter parsing for the Xiaohongshu skill by encoding the description as a YAML block scalar, ensuring valid syntax. The parsing now utilizes `yaml.safe_load` in contract tests, aligning with production behavior. Validation results show all tests passed, confirming the robustness of the changes.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Workflows for Full User Experience in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update expands the Xiaohongshu Skill to support comprehensive workflows including login, recommendations, and content publishing. Key changes include the introduction of explicit high-impact capabilities like file uploads and cookie management, ensuring all actions occur in the user's local browser with strict origin checks. The validation process confirmed the robustness of these enhancements with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: New Browser Tools for Enhanced Publishing in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces explicitly gated browser tools, including file upload and cookie clearing capabilities, enhancing the aichat2 browser functionality. Legacy trusted input remains limited, ensuring security and compliance with existing policies. Comprehensive testing confirms that all changes are stable and ready for deployment.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Uploads with Browser Capabilities in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces explicit browser capabilities for Xiaohongshu uploads, requiring both `file_upload` and `clear_cookies` permissions. The changes ensure that old devices fail selection until re-paired, enhancing security and functionality. All tests have passed successfully, confirming the robustness of the new implementation.

items.title: Enhance Xiaohongshu Publishing Tools in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces locally approved publishing tools, including `browser.file_upload` and `browser.clear_cookies`, to the Ace Data Cloud extension. It ensures that sensitive details are kept memory-only and validates targets post-approval. The extension is now version 0.6.0, featuring enhanced security measures and explicit device capabilities for uploads and cookie resets.

items.title: Enhance Extension Enablement Without Panic Blocks in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the extension's ability to enable without blocking during panic cleanup. Key changes include immediate updates to local runtime state, independent panic recovery alarms, and refined handling of host match patterns to prevent unnecessary permission removals. The validation process confirmed that all tests passed successfully, ensuring a robust implementation.

items.title: Enhance Taskbar Pinning Functionality in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of the Windows 10 LTSC compositor-rendered taskbar jump-list by implementing precise OCR for the 'Pin to taskbar' label. It ensures that the system fails safely on OCR misses and retains checks for pinned shortcuts. Validation tests have passed successfully, confirming the robustness of the changes.

items.title: Sync veo resource with latest Docs spec in S D K 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the `veo` OpenAPI spec with recent changes, including the removal of `veo2` and `veo2-fast` models. The `translation` field has been changed from a string to a boolean, and the `aspect_ratio` is now restricted to only `16:9` and `9:16`. Both TypeScript and Python SDKs have been updated accordingly to reflect these changes.

items.title: Introducing Reusable Email Campaign Templates in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces per-campaign Tencent templates with reusable, versioned templates that are keyed by message class and locale. Key features include a streamlined unsubscribe process, enhanced content validation, and improved template management. The changes ensure that campaigns maintain a consistent structure while allowing for localized content delivery.

items.title: Enhance Referral Risk Admin Page Functionality in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update explicitly registers all Element Plus components used in the referral risk admin page, resolving a critical issue that caused a blank production page. The fix addresses the problem of unknown table components invoking row slots without scope, ensuring a smoother user experience. Validation steps included a successful Vite production build and thorough testing with Playwright.

items.title: Configure Canonical X402 API Host in X402 Client 🚀
items.summary: This update explicitly configures the canonical API host `https://x402.acedata.cloud` across all TypeScript and Python quick-start clients. It aligns the shared agent example client with the dedicated X402 host, preventing fallback to the legacy default. All SDK constructors have been verified to include the new host, ensuring a seamless integration experience.

items.title: Set X402 as Default Gateway in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update establishes `https://x402.acedata.cloud` as the default canonical origin for X402 discovery resources. It includes a refresh of the discovery cache key, enhanced unit testing for canonical URL generation, and updates to the X402 source documentation to reflect these changes. The modifications ensure a more reliable and consistent experience for users accessing X402 resources.

items.title: Enhance Tab Grouping Functionality in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new feature that allows the ACE Browser Agent to reuse tab groups across all attached tabs within the same Chrome window while maintaining separate groups in different windows. Key improvements include the consolidation of legacy tab groups and better handling of closed tabs or invalidated group IDs, ensuring a smoother user experience. Comprehensive validation has been completed with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Revamp Applications Menu to Balance Management in Frontend Web 🎉
items.summary: The applications sidebar item in the console has been renamed to 'Balance Management' and localized across all 12 supported languages. This update ensures that the terminology is consistent and accurately describes the functionality related to global and dedicated application balances, while keeping the page title and route unchanged.

items.title: Update error handling for Nano failures in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the error handling for MountSea Nano task failures, changing the mapping of upstream error code `451` from `ForbiddenError` (HTTP 403) to `ApiError` (HTTP 500). This allows the production Nano failover router to continue to the next eligible channel instead of halting at MountSea, ensuring better resilience in error scenarios. The implementation remains within the existing inline error-mapping branch without introducing new abstractions.

items.title: Enhance Veo Task Details in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enriches the Veo generation task details by adding reference-image thumbnails above each task prompt, aligning with the design of Nano Banana and Seedance history cards. The ambiguous tabs have been replaced with localized names, and a consistent metadata panel is now displayed for all task statuses. Legacy task history remains readable, ensuring a smooth transition for users.

items.title: New Referral Risk Management Console in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a superuser-only referral risk management page at /console/admin/distribution-risk. It features comprehensive histories of blocked, bypassed, and compensated referrals, along with live compensation previews. The page is linked from the admin navigation and supports multiple languages, enhancing usability for global users.

items.title: Enhance Recovery for Ace Data Cloud Extension in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the recovery process for the Ace Data Cloud extension after a stop event. Key changes include preserving required permissions, renaming 'Paused' to 'Stopped', and enhancing the Enable functionality to retry cleanup and reconnect sessions. The extension now handles errors more effectively, providing clearer guidance for users.

items.title: Enhance Referral Risk Decision Auditing in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces significant enhancements to the referral risk decision auditing process. Key changes include the persistence of blocked, bypassed, and compensated decisions, along with new APIs for managing bypass approvals. Additionally, live compensation previews and a robust reconciliation process have been implemented to improve accuracy and transparency in compensation handling.

items.title: Launch Formal Email Campaign Templates in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces formal bilingual email campaign templates with Ace Data Cloud branding, ensuring accessibility and compatibility across email clients. Key enhancements include a centralized subscription topic management and the rejection of ServiceNotice campaigns with declared topics. Extensive testing confirms the stability and functionality of the new templates.

items.title: Display Site Favicons in Browser Agent in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the rendering of Chrome-provided favicons for each attached origin in the Browser Agent. A text fallback is included for instances where a favicon fails to load, enhancing user experience. Additionally, the narrow `favicon` extension permission has been added and validated, ensuring compliance with security policies.

items.title: Revoked browser devices can now be permanently deleted in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new irreversible 'Delete' action for revoked browser devices, ensuring that users can permanently remove devices after revocation. The backend has been updated to support this functionality, and all relevant locales have been backfilled with the necessary translations. Additionally, the UI has been adjusted to align with the Ace Data Cloud branding.

items.title: Updated Email Marketing Sender to Noreply in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update migrates the email marketing identity to `email.acedata.cloud` and sets `noreply@email.acedata.cloud` as the default From address. Key changes include enhanced validation for sender registration and updates to the operations runbook to reflect new DNS settings. The implementation ensures compliance with Tencent SES requirements, improving email campaign reliability.

items.title: Enhance Browser Device Management with Re-Pairing and Deletion in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for re-pairing browser devices by allowing a new pending claim from the same user and installation IDs. It also adds a permanent delete endpoint for revoked devices, ensuring that pairing audit claims are preserved while deleting the associated connections. Comprehensive tests confirm that all changes are functioning as intended.

items.title: Enhance Tab Management and Pairing in Ace Data Cloud Extension 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a more intuitive paired state display, hiding the pairing form once paired. Users can now easily clear local sessions and initiate a new pairing flow with the 'Pair new' option. Additionally, the extension now lists all attached origins with a 'Detach' option for each session, improving user control and experience.

items.title: Email Marketing Access for Superusers in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update exposes the Email Marketing admin entry directly to superusers on the main Ace Data Cloud host, removing the need for a hidden feature flag. The admin route is now accessible without restrictions, while keeping sub-official and white-label hosts hidden. This change enhances the visibility and usability of the email marketing console for superusers.

items.title: Enhance tool name handling for LLM requests in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a fix for handling invalid tool names in LLM requests, specifically addressing the issue with dots in tool names. The solution encodes invalid names into fixed-length aliases while preserving valid names and contracts. Comprehensive validation confirms the effectiveness of these changes across various scenarios.

items.title: Update Branding for Xiaohongshu Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update modifies the branding in the Xiaohongshu Skill to refer to the product as the 'Ace Data Cloud extension'. The existing manual 'Attach current tab' workflow and all read-only safety boundaries remain unchanged. Validation tests for both Xiaohongshu and the Skills repository have passed successfully.

items.title: Hide refresh icon during loading in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the refresh icon while the channel request is loading, enhancing the user experience by relying solely on the Element Plus loading spinner. The circular button's size, tooltip, and accessible name remain unchanged to ensure consistency. All validations, including ESLint and production build, have passed successfully.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Window Management in Wisdom 🚀
items.summary: This update restores hidden WeChat windows from the system tray and ensures the taskbar button remains pinned. Key changes include improved handling of window states and persistent checks for taskbar pinning, enhancing user experience. The modifications have been validated with extensive tests, confirming stability and functionality.

items.title: Updated Referral Commission in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The maximum referral commission in the Studio overview has been corrected from 25% to 24%. This change aligns the public guide with the live 13-tier distribution API, ensuring accurate information for users. The update has been validated against the live API and confirmed to be clean.

items.title: Veo CLI is in perfect sync with Docs in Clis 🚀
items.summary: Recent updates to the documentation for the `veo` service have been verified, confirming that no changes are needed in the CLI. All components, including models, aspect ratios, resolutions, actions, endpoints, and task actions, have been checked and are in alignment. With all 58 tests passing and the linting clean, the CLI remains robust and ready for use.

items.title: Enhance Error Handling for Gemini Upstream in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves error classification for Gemini/Omni upstream validation failures, mapping them to appropriate HTTP status codes. Key changes include handling moderation failures as 403 and timeouts as 504, along with new regression tests for the 10-second video limit. The modifications ensure clearer error messaging and better user experience.

items.title: Streamline Project Skills into Commands in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates all project workflow skills into a single directory, removing duplicates and enhancing organization. Seven unique workflows have been migrated to command files, while five existing commands remain intact. Additionally, command routing and internationalization guidance have been updated to ensure clarity and functionality.

items.title: Enhance Router Response Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the router's handling of managed task responses by ensuring a single terminal JSON response is requested. It normalizes legacy JSON sequences and reuses the final response for task persistence and billing records. Additionally, it filters out unnecessary headers, enhancing compatibility and reliability across various worker services.

items.title: Optimize Marketing Channel Loading for Better Performance in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the automatic loading of marketing channels by ensuring sequential processing to reduce database contention. Key changes include maintaining the automatic state on initial entry and date changes, and canceling queued summaries when filters or date sequences change. The improvements lead to faster analytics completion times and a smoother user experience.

items.title: Enhance Phone Authentication Experience in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the phone authentication process by enhancing captcha feedback and password guidance. It skips captcha for resolved non-official sites while ensuring that official and unresolved callers still require it. Additionally, the canonical site origin is now passed across all SMS/email code-send surfaces, streamlining the user experience.

items.title: Streamline Captcha Process for Registered Subsites in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a significant change by skipping captcha requirements for registered non-official Site origins when issuing SMS/email codes. The system will still require captcha for unknown or official Site contexts, while maintaining per-receiver throttling and implementing a new subsite limit. Comprehensive tests have been added to ensure functionality and security.

items.title: Enhance Referral Risk Audit and Allowlist Design in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a comprehensive design for the referral risk audit and allowlist, focusing on documenting invalid referral-risk audit histories and implementing a directed, append-only bypass system. Key changes include low-lock idempotency, invalid aggregate filtering, and improved reconciliation processes, ensuring a more robust and transparent referral system.

items.title: Automate Marketing Channel Loading in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces automatic loading of channel attribution on the initial dashboard entry and when the reporting date range changes. It optimizes performance by running the channel request in parallel with core analytics, ensuring fast KPI and funnel rendering. Additionally, an explicit refresh option is retained for filter changes to enhance user interaction.

items.title: Enhance App Customization Features in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces a compact edit action for customizing app names and icons on a per-site basis within the Settings > Capabilities section. It leverages existing image upload and translation functionalities, ensuring a seamless user experience across all Navigator surfaces. Additionally, it includes typed CRUD operators and comprehensive tests to maintain code quality and localization support.

items.title: Enhance Site Capabilities with Custom Branding in Backend 🎨
items.summary: This update introduces the `SiteCapabilityOverride` model, allowing for per-site branding with localized display names and custom icon URLs. It includes site-admin CRUD operations, strict HTTPS validation, and improved translation lifecycle management. The changes are additive and backward-compatible, ensuring a smooth rollout before the Nexior consumer PR.

items.title: Standardize Rate Limit Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update maps third-party provider HTTP 429 responses to server-side 500 errors for various adapters including OpenAI HK, ephone, seek, TTAPI, and Volcengine. It preserves intentional public 429 responses for Midjourney Relax queue errors while ensuring internal throttling remains unchanged. The changes enhance error handling consistency across the platform.

items.title: Align GPT-Image-2 Routing for Consistency in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the routing for `gpt-image-2:official` with the existing `gpt-image-2` by routing both through the same `br -> br-fallback` pool. The official alias will no longer dispatch to cortex/openai-hk, ensuring routing parity for image generations and edits. The public model name and billing remain unchanged, maintaining stability for users.

items.title: Enhance OpenAI HK Error Handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update classifies the OpenAI HK provider's capacity-saturation response as a `500 api_error`, while preserving ordinary upstream rate-limit responses as `429 too_many_requests`. Additionally, it normalizes Unicode and whitespace to ensure minor formatting changes are correctly classified. These improvements enhance error handling and maintain system reliability.

items.title: Eliminate Polling for Removed Desktop Agent Endpoint in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update removes the hidden Desktop Agent manager state and the associated polling mechanism that was hitting a deleted endpoint, resulting in unnecessary 404 errors. The Browser Agent execution remains unaffected, ensuring smooth functionality. All relevant references and calls have been cleaned up, and validation checks confirm the integrity of the remaining code.

items.title: Revamp Browser Agent Side Panel with ACE Design in Frontend Web 🎨
items.summary: This update aligns the Browser Agent side panel with ACE design standards by importing core design tokens and controls. Key changes include the replacement of standalone utility styling with ACE brand patterns, improved state distinctions, and enhanced accessibility. The extension has passed all validation checks, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Revamped Browser Session Connections in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the rendering of browser session connections, displaying them as local browser devices with detailed information such as device name, status, platform, and more. It removes unnecessary metadata while preserving existing OAuth and other connection types. The changes improve user experience by providing clearer and more relevant connection details.

items.title: Enhance Connection Responses with Safe Browser Device Summaries in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a read-only `browser_device` summary to Connection responses, ensuring key information remains secure. The implementation preloads BrowserDevice to optimize query performance and maintains null fields for standard OAuth Connections. Comprehensive tests confirm the stability and efficiency of these changes.
